steps.title: Find the y-intercept by setting x = 0
steps.content: The y-intercept occurs where the graph crosses the y-axis, which happens when $x = 0$. Substitute this value into the equation $y = 10^x + 22$.

steps.title: Calculate the y-value
steps.content:
$$y = 10^0 + 22$$
Since $10^0 = 1$:
$$y = 1 + 22 = 23$$
The y-intercept is at the point $(0, 23)$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
